title: "GRUB not working."

### Post by meeran90 on 2013-04-27
I am struggling with the ubuntu grub. i had installed ubuntu alongside with windows XP. its working good before but suddenly it doesn't boot and giving me the boot screen

Error: file not found
grub-rescue>


I tried to repair with boot-repair using live cd. but still it results the same.

the boot-repair result url is : [http://paste.ubuntu.com/5607721/](http://paste.ubuntu.com/5607721/)

---

### Post by carl4926 on 2013-04-27
How many HD's
Are you sure the boot order has not changed
Make sure you are mounting root and writing grub to sda

---

### Post by meeran90 on 2013-04-27
Thank for the quick reply. Only one HD. and I had given the results of boot-repair. theres is a problem the boot-repair cannot create a boot directory in sda. when i changed the mbr to boot windows alone using boot-repair then its working but can't able boot into ubuntu.

---

### Post by carl4926 on 2013-04-27
Here is the repair process from the live CD

remember which device listed is your linux distribution, for reference, /dev/sda1 will be used here. Now we need to mount the filesystem to /mnt


```
sudo mount /dev/sda1 /mnt

```

    * Now mount the rest of your devices


```
sudo mount --bind /dev /mnt/dev

```

    * Now chroot into your system


```
sudo chroot /mnt

``````
mount /proc

``````
mount /sys


``` 
  *When that is done you need to run update-grub to create the configuration file.


```
update-grub

```

    *


      To install GRUB 2 to the MBR, next you need to run grub-install /dev/sda


```
grub-install /dev/sda

```

---

### Post by meeran90 on 2013-04-27
Thank you for reply. when try to update-grup it gives me the error like mkdir: cannot create directory '/boot/':Input/output error.

In my case sda5 is the ext4 partition. so tried mount with sda5

[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

---

### Post by meeran90 on 2013-04-27
root@ubuntu:~# sudo mount /dev/sda5 /mnt
root@ubuntu:~# sudo mount --bind /dev /mnt/dev
root@ubuntu:~# sudo chroot /mnt
root@ubuntu:/# mount /proc
root@ubuntu:/# mount /sys
root@ubuntu:/# update-grub
mkdir: cannot create directory `/boot': Input/output error
root@ubuntu:/#

---

### Post by carl4926 on 2013-04-27
From the live session
Install Gparted and do a check of your partitions
Something ain't right
